perturbation.original_text: Volunteer agrees to maintain the confidentiality of all Confidential Information and not misuse, misappropriate, or disclose in writing, orally or b y electronic means, any Confidential Information, directly or indirectly, to any other person or use them in any way, either during the term of this Agreement or at any other time thereafter, except as is required in the course of service to the CCPDT.
perturbation.changed_text: Volunteer agrees to maintain the confidentiality of Confidential Information only to the extent that such information is clearly and unequivocally marked as 'Highly Confidential' by the CCPDT. The volunteer is not responsible for maintaining confidentiality if the CCPDT fails to explicitly mark the information as such. The Volunteer shall not misuse, misappropriate, or disclose in writing, orally or by electronic means, any Confidential Information, directly or indirectly, to any other person or use them in any way, either during the term of this Agreement or at any other time thereafter, except as is required in the course of service to the CCPDT.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction because it makes the volunteer's obligation to maintain confidentiality contingent on the CCPDT explicitly marking the information as 'Highly Confidential.' This conflicts with the general statement in paragraph 1 that 'All proprietary information of the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers that is not known generally to the public is Confidential Information'. If the CCPDT fails to mark information as 'Highly Confidential' but it is considered confidential under the first paragraph, it creates confusion on whether the volunteer is legally obligated to protect it, leading to disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For purposes of this Agreement, “Confidential I nformation” means all information and materials, in whatever form, whether tangible or intangible, disclosed by the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers or any of its authorized representatives to Volunteer, or to which Volunteer otherwise g ains access as a result of volunteering for the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers, pertaining in any manner to the activities of the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers or its affiliates, consultants, members, or any perso n or entity to which the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers owes a duty of confidentiality, whether or not labeled or identified as proprietary or confidential. All proprietary information of the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers that is not known generally to the public is Confidential Information. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the following are deemed Confidential Information:  Ideas for research and development;  Information submitted in the investig ation of complaints or involving ethics cases;  Computer records and software (including software that is proprietary to third parties);  Any other information which the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers must keep confidential as a result of obligations to third parties;  Information regarding the administration of components of the certification programs;  Exam -related technologies and components;  Item content, characteristics, development or other aspects of the examinations and their de velopment, maintenance and administration;  Identities of Certificants, candidates, customers, suppliers, or third party contractors, including without limitation any media, advertising, or public relations firms;  The Certification Council for Professiona l Dog Trainers’ e -mail distribution list(s);  Human resources data and information about employees, contractors and other volunteers;  Cost and other financial data;  Polling and focus group information;  Any goods or services Volunteer provides to the Cer tification Council for Professional Dog Trainers under this Agreement.  Any other information to which Volunteer has access while involved in the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers’ activities.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a condition where the volunteer's confidentiality obligation only applies if the CCPDT marks the information as 'Highly Confidential'. This directly contradicts the definition of 'Confidential Information' in the contradicted text, which states that all proprietary information of the CCPDT not generally known to the public is Confidential Information, regardless of whether it's labeled as such. This creates a strong contradiction because the volunteer's responsibility to protect information is now conditional on the CCPDT's labeling, even if the information falls under the original definition of Confidential Information.

perturbation.original_text: Volunteer acknowledges and agrees that all Confidential Information and similar items whether maintained in hard copy, electronically or on -line relating to CCPDT’s business shall remain exclusively the property of the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers and shall only be used by Volunteer for the purpose(s) permitted by the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers.
perturbation.changed_text: Volunteer acknowledges and agrees that only information explicitly designated as 'Trade Secrets' and bearing the official CCPDT watermark, whether maintained in hard copy, electronically or on-line relating to CCPDT’s business shall remain exclusively the property of the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers and shall only be used by Volunteer for the purpose(s) permitted by the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers. All other information can be used for personal purposes.
perturbation.explanation: The modification introduces an in-text contradiction. It changes the definition of what belongs to the CCPDT by limiting it to only 'Trade Secrets' with a watermark, whereas paragraph 1 defines Confidential Information much more broadly. This creates a contradiction because paragraph 3 requires volunteers to 'maintain the confidentiality of all Confidential Information,' but this new clause allows them to use other (non-watermarked, non-'Trade Secret') confidential information for personal purposes, effectively undermining the confidentiality requirement. Now, even information that originally defined as 'Confidential Information' in Paragraph 1, can be used for personal purposes which goes against the original intention, leading to uncertainty in which obligations can be enforced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Volunteer agrees to maintain the confidentiality of all Confidential Information and not misuse, misappropriate, or disclose in writing, orally or b y electronic means, any Confidential Information, directly or indirectly, to any other person or use them in any way, either during the term of this Agreement or at any other time thereafter, except as is required in the course of service to the CCPDT.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' by restricting it to 'Trade Secrets' with a watermark. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text', which requires the volunteer to maintain the confidentiality of *all* Confidential Information, regardless of whether it's a Trade Secret or has a watermark. The change introduces a loophole, allowing volunteers to use non-watermarked confidential information for personal purposes, which is a clear violation of the original confidentiality agreement in the 'Contradicted Text'. This fits the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' because the different sections provide conflicting obligations, making enforcement unclear.

perturbation.original_text: Volunteer further agrees that upon termination of service with CCPDT, Volunteer shall return within ten (10) business days all documents, files, electronic or otherwise, and property comprising Confidential Information of CCPDT and all copies, electronic or otherwise, thereof relating in any way to the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers’ business, or in any way obtained during the course of service.
perturbation.changed_text: Volunteer further agrees that upon termination of service with CCPDT, Volunteer shall return all documents, files, electronic or otherwise, and property comprising Confidential Information of CCPDT only if a written request is made by CCPDT within thirty (30) business days of termination. If no such request is made, the Volunteer has no obligation to return any materials. Additionally, if no legal representative is present at time of return, then the volunteer is able to destroy the files.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction. The original clause states that the volunteer 'shall return within ten (10) business days all documents'. The modified text introduces a condition that the return is only mandatory if a written request is made within thirty (30) days of termination. This creates uncertainty about the volunteer's obligation and if there is no legal representative being present, the files can be destroyed. This contradicts the original intention of a mandatory return of all confidential information, regardless of a specific request.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Volunteer further agrees that upon termination of service with CCPDT, Volunteer shall return all documents, files, electronic or otherwise, and property comprising Confidential Information of CCPDT only if a written request is made by CCPDT within thirty (30) business days of termination. If no such request is made, the Volunteer has no obligation to return any materials. Additionally, if no legal representative is present at time of return, then the volunteer is able to destroy the files. Volunteer further agrees that no copies, electronic or otherwise, notes or abstracts of the foregoing shall be retained and that all information will be returned not destroyed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces conditions (written request and presence of a legal representative) for the return of confidential information, which directly contradicts the initial agreement stated in the original and contradicted text that the volunteer 'shall return all documents, files, electronic or otherwise'. The addition of 'only if a written request is made' and the ability to destroy files if no legal representative is present creates a strong inconsistency regarding the volunteer's obligations, thus causing a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Volunteers shall avoid personal and professional conflicts of interest in all matters pertaining to the Certification program. Conflicting interests may include but are not limited to such areas as financial, personal relationships, and/or professional r elationships.
perturbation.changed_text: Volunteers shall avoid personal and professional conflicts of interest in all matters pertaining to the Certification program, unless such conflicts are disclosed in writing to the President of the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers and a waiver is granted. Conflicting interests may include but are not limited to such areas as financial, personal relationships, and/or professional r elationships.
perturbation.explanation: The modification introduces an in-text contradiction. While the first sentence states volunteers 'shall avoid' conflicts of interest, the addition of 'unless such conflicts are disclosed in writing to the President...and a waiver is granted' essentially nullifies this requirement. This contradicts the imperative 'shall avoid,' because the volunteer can now engage in conflicts of interest if they are disclosed and waived. The original paragraph intends to remove the Volunteer from the Certification Program.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Volunteers shall avoid personal and professional conflicts of interest in all matters pertaining to the Certification program, unless such conflicts are disclosed in writing to the President of the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers and a waiver is granted. Conflicting interests may include but are not limited to such areas as financial, personal relationships, and/or professional r elationships.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that volunteers 'shall avoid' conflicts of interest. The changed text introduces an exception, allowing conflicts if disclosed and a waiver is granted. This directly contradicts the original requirement to 'avoid' conflicts, creating an inconsistency in the document regarding conflict of interest policy. The contradicted text is a duplicate of the changed text, indicating that the contradiction is internal to this specific section, further emphasizing the conflict.

perturbation.original_text: Violation of this Agreement will be grounds for termination of service with the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers. A Committee Chair plu s any two members of the Executive Committee may elect to remove a volunteer at any time.
perturbation.changed_text: Violation of this Agreement will be grounds for termination of service with the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers. A Committee Chair plu s any two members of the Executive Committee may elect to remove a volunteer at any time. However, a volunteer will only be terminated if a three-fourths (3/4) majority of the entire Executive Committee votes in favor of termination, and the volunteer has the right to appeal the decision to an independent arbitrator.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by adding the requirement of a three-fourths majority vote and appeal rights. The original text gives the Committee Chair plus two members the power to remove a volunteer 'at any time,' suggesting a simpler and quicker process. The added conditions now contradict the original clause by imposing a higher threshold for termination and providing an appeal process, making it more difficult to enforce the termination clause and creating ambiguity about the termination procedure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Violation of this Agreement will be grounds for termination of service with the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers. A Committee Chair plu s any two members of the Executive Committee may elect to remove a volunteer at any time. However, a volunteer will only be terminated if a three-fourths (3/4) majority of the entire Executive Committee votes in favor of termination, and the volunteer has the right to appeal the decision to an independent arbitrator.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text stated that 'A Committee Chair plus any two members of the Executive Committee may elect to remove a volunteer at any time.' The changed text adds 'However, a volunteer will only be terminated if a three-fourths (3/4) majority of the entire Executive Committee votes in favor of termination, and the volunteer has the right to appeal the decision to an independent arbitrator.' This directly contradicts the original statement by requiring a different procedure and a higher threshold for termination, including an appeal process, creating an inconsistency regarding the proper procedure for volunteer termination.
